question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests with alternating order=asc and order=desc to prevent the database query planner from caching execution plans, causing repeated plan recompilation that delays block processing by 500% of the 24-hour average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.getInclusiveRangeValue() arithmetic by supplying a GT operator with value=Long.MAX_VALUE-1, causing value+1 to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE and producing an invalid range that triggers unexpected full-table scans delaying block processing by 500%?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.adjustUpperBound() decrement by supplying a LT operator with value=Long.MIN_VALUE, causing upperBound-- to underflow and produce Long.MAX_VALUE, resulting in an unbounded scan that delays block processing by 500%?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Bound.getAdjustedLowerRangeValue() increment by supplying a GT operator with value=Long.MAX_VALUE, causing lowerBound++ to overflow to Long.MIN_VALUE, bypassing the lower > upper validation check and triggering an unbounded scan?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with no account.id or token.id parameters (both Bound.EMPTY), causing NftAllowanceRequest.getBounds() to return a list with a single EMPTY Bound, which may trigger an unfiltered query against NFT_ALLOWANCE that returns all rows for the given account and delays block processing?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PageRequest.of(0, limit, sort) construction with limit=100 to force the service to always fetch from page 0 with maximum page size, preventing any server-side cursor optimization and causing repeated expensive full-range queries that delay block processing by 500%?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with account.id=eq:0 (zero entity ID) to force a query against a potentially hot partition of NFT_ALLOWANCE, causing lock contention with concurrent writes and delaying block transaction processing by 500% of the average block time?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the multi-column Sort.by(order, ACCOUNT_ID, TOKEN_ID) with a wide account.id range and limit=100 to force the database to perform an expensive multi-key sort that saturates CPU and delays block processing by 500% of the 24-hour average?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/controller/AllowancesController.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: getNftAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a 3-part entity ID path variable (e.g., /api/v1/accounts/0.0.1/allowances/nfts) that resolves to a system account with a large number of NFT allowances, causing expensive queries that delay block processing by 500%?
